claim_text: On the strict n=15 AX ladder invD_chosen is a stronger empirical predictor of the primary A targets than Xeff_chosen under the audited LOOCV / log-log metrics.
forbidden_extension: invD is uniquely correct physics; Xeff is wrong
notes: AX_17B judgement; AX_18C coord rows; AX_19A ranks.

claim_text: invD_power (empirical log-log template in AX-18C) achieves lower LOOCV_RMSE in A space than T_linear for both targets.
required_caveat: Alpha descriptive only; endpoint stability not recorded

claim_text: T_power and Xeff_power single-term templates do not beat T_linear on the same AX-18C scoring rule.
forbidden_extension: Therefore Xeff or T never matter
notes: Does not exclude linear-in-Xeff models (different class).

claim_text: Physical scaling law or mechanism for A(invD) is established.
invD replaces X_eff as the dimensionless paper variable without qualification.
required_caveat: X_eff remains canonical ratio label per P0

claim_text: X_eff explains high-T turnover / peak alignment better than invD on audited shape metrics.
required_caveat: invD aligns peak with A ~29 K vs Xeff ~25 K offset

claim_text: After linear control for best T proxy (T_relax_raw) log(invD) still improves log(A) LOOCV vs T alone; log(Xeff) does not on AX-19A partial table.
Temperature retains incremental information beyond invD (best T adds beyond invD in AX-18C; best_T_improves_beyond_invD in AX-19A).
Empirical invD_power exponents are approximately 0.56 for both targets (0.562460847 and 0.558279495).
All simple scaling laws are ruled out.
No tested candidate establishes a physical scaling law.
